title: Ginger Charred Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A charred, creamy, and festive Algerian dish featuring Vin Santo and infused oil, perfect for a special occasion. This recipe is a Mediterranean-inspired delight that's easy to make and serves a crowd.

ingredients.name: boneless lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: preferably with the fat cap on

instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and paprika.
Heat the infused oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the chopped onion to the pot. Cook until the onion is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chickpeas, ginger, and Vin Santo to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the lamb until tender and falling apart, about 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Use two forks to shred the lamb into bite-sized pieces.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
